def: s.f. = majīṭh, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī) = maṅjhlā = majhlā, q.v.

ety: māṅj˚ = Prk. मंज(इ), or मंजे(इ) = S. मार्जय(ति), fr. मार्ज्, or fr. rt. मृज्;+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To rub, scrub, scour, clean, furbish, polish
s.m. = mājnā, q.v.

ety: fr. the trans. māṅjnā, q.v.
def: v.n. To be rubbed, or wiped, or scoured, or cleaned, or polished, or smoothed.
adj. See منجو manju.
adj. Beautiful, lovely, charming, pleasing, pleasant, agreeable, sweet; soft, melodious (i.q. manjul).

adj. (f. -ā), Beautiful, pleasing, charming, lovely, pleasant, agreeable, sweet; soft, melodious; — s.m. A species of water-hen or gallinule; — a bower, an arbour; — a spring-well; a natural water-course.
adj. & s.m. corr. of manjul, q.v.

ety: fr. manjul, q.v.
def: s.f. Beautifulness, loveliness, agreeableness, &c.
s.f. A large basket; a basket; a box, chest; a receptacle.

ety: S. मणिक+कः
def: s.m. A bead (of gold, or silver, &c.); a rosary; — the 'Adam's apple'; the os coccygis; a single vertebra (of the back or neck); — an amulet worn round the neck
subs: 'The vertebræ of the neck to incline or bend,' to be dying, to be at the point of death.

ety: for A. منقبة, v.n. fr. نقب; see manqab
def: s.f. Anything in which a man glories or which confers on him pre-eminence; glory; ability, accomplishment; praise (esp. of the Prophet and his companions).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مَنْقَبَة (manqaba).

ety: n. of instr. fr. نقل 'to transport,' &c.
def: s.m. A chafing-dish.

ety: for A. منقلة manqalat, n. of place fr. نقل 'to transport,' &c.
def: s.m. A way through mountains; — a day's journey, or the space travelled; a halting-place.
s.f. See s.v. man.
s.m. = H من کرم بانی मन क्रम बानी man-kram-bānī, s.f. (dialec.) = mansā-vāćā-karmaṇā, q.v.s.v. mansā.
s.f. = H من کرم بچن मन क्रम बचन man-kram-baćan, s.m. (dialec.) = mansā-vāćā-karmaṇā, q.v.s.v. mansā.

ety: for A. منکوحة mankūḥat, fem. of mankūḥ, pass. part. of نکح 'to marry,' &c.
def: part. Married (woman); — s.f. A married woman; a lawfully married wife.

ety: pass. part. of نقل 'to translate; to transcribe; to relate,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Translated; transcribed, copied; — said; related, narrated, recounted; handed down by tradition; — adopted (a word); desumed, borrowed (as the science of the fundamentals of religion, and the like; — in this sense opposed to maʻqūl, q.v.); — (property) that may be removed, movable, or personal (property); — technical; — manqūla, s.m. That which is (or has been) said, or related; saying; tradition; — movable or personal property; — an adopted word; a borrowed science, &c.

ety: for A. منقولة manqūlat, fem. of manqūl
def: part. adj. & s.m. Translated; transcribed, copied; — said; related, narrated, recounted; handed down by tradition; — adopted (a word); desumed, borrowed (as the science of the fundamentals of religion, and the like; — in this sense opposed to maʻqūl, q.v.); — (property) that may be removed, movable, or personal (property); — technical; — manqūla, s.m. That which is (or has been) said, or related; saying; tradition; — movable or personal property; — an adopted word; a borrowed science, &c.
s.m. pl. (of manqūlat), Things related; sayings; traditions; — movables, &c. (see manqūla).

ety: pass. part. of نقش 'to paint,' &c.
def: part. Painted; coloured; stained; printed; — embroidered; — carved; engraved.

ety: pass. part. of نقط 'to mark (a letter
def: part. Spotted, dotted; marked with a diacritical point (as ذ, ز, &c.); — s.m. A species of composition in which all the letters have diacritical points
subs: v.t. To mark (or distinguish) with a dot or dots, to dot.

ety: for A. منقوطة manqūt̤at, fem. of manqūt̤
def: part. Spotted, dotted; marked with a diacritical point (as ذ, ز, &c.); — s.m. A species of composition in which all the letters have diacritical points
subs: v.t. To mark (or distinguish) with a dot or dots, to dot.

def: s.m. Love, amorous passion or desire; lust; — the god of love, Cupid (i.q. Kām-dev); — the elephant or wood-apple, Feronia elephantum.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Enamoured, impassioned, amorous, in love; — one who is in love (with).
adj. & s.m. See s.v. man, 'mind.'
s.m. A species of honey-dew gathered from plants, manna
subs: or mann-salwā, s.m. Manna and quails (wherewith the Israelites were fed in the wilderness).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian من (man), ultimately from Akkadian 𒈠𒈾 (manûm).

def: v.t. prop. ماننا mānnā, q.v.

ety: mān˚ = Prk. मान(इ), or माने(इ) = S. मानय(ति), caus. fr. rt. मन्;+nā = Prk. अनअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To respect, revere, esteem; to regard, heed, mind, attend to, observe, obey; to believe, trust, credit; to admit, allow, acknowledge, confess, own; to permit; to acknowledge the superiority of, to submit, yield; to agree to, assent to; to consent; to accept, receive; to take, assume, suppose; to take for granted, to grant; to hold, view, consider, regard as, deem, account; to hold to be true or right; to consider of importance; to approve; — to feel, experience, entertain; — to be set on
subs: (intens.) To heed, mind, &c.; to acknowledge the superiority of; to submit, yield, come to terms with; to comply with; to acknowledge, confess, own, &c.
adj. Worthy of honour or veneration, venerable, &c.; worthy of acceptance or belief, credible, &c.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀫𑀡𑁆𑀡𑀤𑀺 (maṇṇadi), from Sanskrit मन्यते (mányate), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *mányatay, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *mányatay, from Proto-Indo-European *mn̥yo-, from *men- (“to think”).

def: v.n. See مننا mannā.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀼𑀁𑀟 (muṃḍa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit मुण्ड (muṇḍa). Cognate with Punjabi ਮੁੰਡਾ (muṇḍā), ਮੁੰਨਾ (munnā).

def: s.m. corr. of mann, 'manna,' q.v.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀼𑀁𑀟 (muṃḍa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit मुण्ड (muṇḍa). Cognate with Punjabi ਮੁੰਡਾ (muṇḍā), ਮੁੰਨਾ (munnā).

ety: see the trans. mānnā, and manānā
def: v.n. To be soothed, to be tranquillized; to be conciliated, or propitiated; — to be invoked.

ety: mannā˚ = Prk. मन्नावे(इ) = S. मन्य(ते)+ आपि caus. augment
def: v.n. To be or become offended or angry; — to raise its hood (a snake).
wety: From Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀫𑀡𑁆𑀡𑀇 (maṇṇaï), from Sanskrit मन्यते (manyate).

ety: mān(nā)+at = Prk. अत्ती = S. आप्ति (āpi, caus. aug.+suff. ti)
def: s.f. Acknowledgment, assent; promise, vow; — desire, intention
subs: To fulfil a vow; to take off amulets, &c. (worn in consequence of a vow) when the vow is fulfilled
(-kī), To make a vow; — to observe or fulfil a vow.

def: s.m. See s.v. mann.

ety: prob. S. माणवकः
def: s.m. (dialec.) A monkey (also written manuʼā; cf. maimūn).

ety: onomat.
def: s.m. A cat.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit मानव (mānava).

ety: 2nd pers. pl. of the aorist of mānnā
def: conj. You would suppose; one might suppose; as if, so to speak, as it were; in effect, virtually; suppose; let it be granted (that, -ki), grant.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit मानव (mānava).

def: conj. Like, as if, as though, &c. (i.q. māno, q.v.).
adj. See s.v. manas.
adj. See s.v. manas.
conj. = mano = māno, qq.v.
conj. = māno, q.v.
s.m. For this and other words beginning with mano that are not found below, see s.v. manas.
= H مانپنا मांपना māṅpnā, dialec. forms of māp, and māpnā, qq.v.
= H مانپ मांप māṅp, dialec. forms of māp, and māpnā, qq.v.
s.m. = māṅḍ, q.v.
s.f. = maṅḍhī = maṛhī, q.v.
s.f. = māṅḍā, q.v.
v.t. = māṅḍnā, q.v.
v.n. (dialec. or rustic) = manḍlānā, q.v.

r: maṅṛwā, maṅṛuʼā, maṅṛwī, maṅṛuʼī, maṅḍwā, maṛuʼā, rāgī
def: s.m. = H منڙوي मंड़वी maṅṛwī, or मंड़ुई maṅṛuʼī, s.f. = maṅḍwā, or maṛuʼā, qq.v. (syn. rāgī).

r: maṅṛwī, maṅṛuʼī, maṅṛwā, maṅṛuʼā, maṅḍwā, maṛuʼā, rāgī
def: s.f. = H منڙوا मंड़वा maṅṛwā, or मंड़ुआ maṅṛuʼā, s.m. = maṅḍwā, or maṛuʼā, qq.v. (syn. rāgī).

ety: Prk. मंसं; S. मांसं
def: s.m. Flesh, meat
subs: or māṅs-ādhārī, or māṅs-āśī, or māṅs-āhārī, or māṅs-bhaksh, adj. & s.m. Flesh-eating, carnivorous; — flesh-eater, carnivorous animal
wety: From Sanskrit मनुष्य (manuṣya), likely from a derivative of Proto-Indo-European *mon- (“man”).

ety: n. of place fr. نصب 'to erect, set up,' &c.
def: s.m. Post, office, station, dignity; ministry; magistracy
subs: s.m. One in office; an officer or official of rank; a functionary; a magistrate
s.f. Office, post, appointment; official position
s.m. A government, or an official, post or position.

ety: fr. manṣab
def: adj. Of or pertaining to office, or official position; official

ety: n. of place fr. نسج 'to weave'
def: s.m. A weaver's shop; — a workshop
subs: (n. of instr. fr. the same verb), s.m.(?) The instrument by which the web is stretched when working; — a weaver's loom; — the part of a horse about the fore-shoulder and under the mane.

def: s.m. (dialec.) = مينسل mainsil, q.v.
= H مانسر मानसुर mānsur, = H مانسرور मानसरवर mānsarwar, s.m. corr. of mānas-sarowar, q.v.s.v. mānas.

ety: Prk. मणूसडओ (with w inserted); S. मनुष्य+र+कः
def: s.m. (rustic) A man, a male (i.q. manseru).
= H مانسر मानसुर mānsur, = H مانسروور मानसरोवर mānsarowar, s.m. corr. of mānas-sarowar, q.v.s.v. mānas.

ety: Ap. Prk. मणूसडउ, Prk. मणूसडओ (with य inserted); S. मनुष्य+र+कः
def: s.m. (rustic) A man, a male (i.q. mansarwā).

ety: the pop. form of A. منشأ manshaʼ, n. of place fr. نشأ 'to grow, to spring up,' &c.
def: s.m. Source, origin, beginning, principle, spring; motive, object, intention, design, purport, tenor, drift, provision (of a law or statute); — allusion; — exordium, or argument (of any composition)
subs: According to the tenor or purport of; in the sense of.

ety: pass. part. of نشر 'to spread, to publish,' &c.
def: part. adj. Diffused, spread abroad, divulged: published (as a book, &c.); — s.m. A royal mandate, diploma, patent, charter (not sealed or stamped with the royal signet); — (in Geom.) a right prism.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَنْشُور (manšūr), from Arabic مَنْشُور (manšūr). First attested in c. 1684 as Middle Hindi منشور (mnśvr⁩ /⁠manśūr⁠/).

ety: pass. part. of نَسِيَ 'to be forgotten,' &c.
def: part. Forgotten, buried in oblivion.

ety: Prk. मंसिआ; S. मांसिका
def: s.f. Indian spikenard, Nardostachys (or Valeriana) jatamansi.
s.m. (dialec.) = مينسل mainsil, q.v.

ety: pass. part. of نسب 'to refer (to
def: part. adj. Connected (with), related (to), belonging (to), relative; depending (on); allied; betrothed; — addicted (to); — denominated (from one's family), surnamed; — deduced (from), derived (from); referred (to), ascribed, attributed, imputed (to); charged (with); chargeable
subs: v.t. To connect, or associate (one, with, -ba); to ascribe, attribute, or impute (to); to charge (with).

ety: pass. part. of نصب 'to erect, set up,' &c.
def: part. adj. Erected, set up, constituted, fixed, determined, established, appointed, nominated, named (for)

ety: for A. منصوبة manṣūbat, fem. of manṣūb
def: s.m. Determination; intention; will, desire; — contrivance, plan, design, project, scheme
subs: adj. & s.m. Considerate, foreseeing, prudent, sagacious, ingenious; — aspiring, ambitious; — scheming, designing, plotting, &c.; — a prudent, or considerate, man, &c.; — a contriver, plotter, schemer, a designing person
manṣūba ṭhahrānā, or manṣūba karnā, To determine, resolve; — to form the plan or design (of), to plan, scheme, contrive, &c.

ety: pass. part. of نسخ 'to cancel,' &c.
def: part. Cancelled, abolished, abrogated, annulled, repealed; effaced, obliterated, erased; broken
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian منسوخ (mansūx), from Arabic مَنْسُوخ (mansūḵ).

ety: fr. mansūḵẖ
def: s.f. Cancelment, abolition, abrogation, annulment, repeal, &c.

ety: pass. part. of نصر 'to aid, help,' &c.
def: part. adj. Aided, succoured, defended, or protected (by God); triumphant; victorious, conquering
= H مانسرور मानसरवर mānsarwar, = H مانسروور मानसरोवर mānsarowar, s.m. corr. of mānas-sarowar, q.v.s.v. mānas.

ety: pass. part. of نصّ 'to manifest,' &c.
def: part. Manifested, declared

ety: Prk. मंत; S. मत्; fem. Prk. मंती; S. मती
def: A suff. used in Hindī to form possessional adjectives from substantives; e.g. sirimant, hanumant, q.v.
s.m. = māṭ, or māṭh, q.v.

ety: S. मान्यता, or मानता, or mān(nā) + at = Prk. अत्ती = S. आप्तिः (i.e. आपि caus. aug.+aff. ति
def: s.f. Obedience; belief, religious belief; — voluntary obligation, vow; promise; resolution, purpose; — acknowledgment, assent, acceptance, acquiescence (syn. mannat.)

s.m. Stirring round, churning; shaking about, agitating; — killing, slaying; — a drink made of barley-meal with ghī and water; a sort of gruel or porridge; — a spoon for stirring; — a churning-stick; — the sun; a ray of the sun; — a disease of the eye, cataract, opacity; — excretion of the eyes, rheum.
s.m. = māṭ, or māṭh, q.v.
= H مانتها मांथा māṅthā, dialec. forms of māthā, q.v.

ety: Prk. मट्ठअं; S. मृष्ट+कं, rt. मृष्
def: adj. Wiped out, effaced; desolate, ruined; non-existent, extinct.
= H مانتهہ मांथ māṅth, dialec. forms of māthā, q.v.
s.m. Shaking, agitating, stirring; churning (i.q. mathan); — injury; slaughter, murder.
s.f. A churning-vessel, churn.

adj. Slow, tardy, lazy, inert, torpid; dull, stupid; low, hollow, deep (as sound, &c.); — crooked, bent, distorted; large, broad, wide; — s.m. A treasure, store; — fruit; — an obstacle, a hindrance; — a churning-stick; — safflower.
part. = mathit, q.v.
s.m. = māṭhū, q.v.
s.m. = māṭī, q.v.

ety: S. मन्त्रिन्+कः; or मस्तिकं
def: s.m. (dialec.), Master.

ety: inf. n. of نطق 'to speak, articulate,' &c.
def: s.f. An oration; — eloquence; — logic, reasoning (i.q. ʻilm-ě-mant̤iq)
subs: or mant̤iq ćhāṅṭnā, v.n. To chop logic.

ety: rel. n. fr. mantiq
def: adj. & s.m. Logical; dialectic; — a logician.

ety: mant̤iq, q.v.+Prk. इओ = S. इकः (इन्+कः)
def: s.m. A logician; one who chops logic; an argumentative person; a litigious person.

s.m. That part of each Veda which comprizes the hymns (as distinguished from the Brāhmaṇas, or theological portions); — a passage of the Vedas, a holy text; — a formula sacred to any particular deity; — a mystical verse, a magical formula; an incantation, a charm, spell, philter; — secret consultation, concerting together; — spiritual instruction (i.q. gur-mantar); counsel, advice
subs: (-par), or mantar paṛh-ke phūknā, or mantar paṛhnā (-par), or mantar-ćalānā, or mantar mārnā, To breathe, or to read or repeat, or to cast, a spell or incantation (over)
vulg. mantra-gya, s.m. A counsellor, adviser; a learned Brāhmaṇ, a priest; — a spy
or mantar-jantar, (S. mantra-yantra), s.m. Incantation, exorcism, sorcery
adj. & s.m. Possessing good counsel; — a counsellor, an adviser
(-ko), To give counsel or advice (to); to give spiritual instruction (to); — to make a disciple (of)
s.m. 'King of spells'; name of a particular magical formula
s.f. The accomplishment, or operation, of a spell; — the effecting or carrying out of a deliberation or advice
(˚tra+up), s.m. The instruction, or formulæ, imparted by a religious teacher.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit मन्त्र (mantra).

def: s.f. The act of consulting, consultation, deliberation; — advising, counselling; counsel; persuasion.
s.m. A king's councillor, minister, adviser, counsellor; an enchanter
part. adj. (f. -ā), Advised, counselled; — initiated or consecrated by the recital of sacred texts or magical formulæ.
s.f. = S منترتو मन्त्रित्व mantritva, s.m. The condition, or office, or functions, of a minister (mantrī), or councillor; ministership, ministry; councillorship.
s.m. = S منترتا मन्त्रिता mantritā, s.f. The condition, or office, or functions, of a minister (mantrī), or councillor; ministership, ministry; councillorship.
s.m. See منو manu.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian من (man), ultimately from Akkadian 𒈠𒈾 (manûm).

def: s.m. A man (in general); the representative man, and father of the human race, Manu, the legislator and saint, son of Brahmā, or personification of Brahmā himself (the name is, however, a generic term; and in every kalpa, or interval from one creation to another, there are fourteen successive Manus presiding over the universe, each of them for a period called manvantara; the first in order of these Manus is called Svayambhuva, as sprung from Svayam-bhū, 'the Self-existent,' and to him is ascribed the celebrated law-book known as the 'code of Manu'; the next five Manus in regular succession are called Svāroćisha, Auttami, Tāmasa, Raivata, Ćākshusha; the seventh Manu, called Vaivasvata or 'Sun-born,' is the Manu of the present period); — a symbolical expression for the number fourteen
subs: vulg. manuj, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Manu-born, sprung from Manu; — a human being, a man
(˚jād = ja+ad), adj. & s.m. (f. -ā or -ī), Man-eating, devouring human beings; — a Rākshas
s.f. The code of laws promulgated by Manu
s.m. The period or age of a Manu (supposed to be equal to 12,000 years of the gods, or 4,320,000 human years).

ety: prob. S. माणवकः or मनु+कः
def: s.m. (dialec. or rustic) A man; — a monkey (i.q. mannū, q.v.).
s.f. (dialec.) A kind of cotton.

ety: dialec.
def: s.m. A cat (prob. fr. the mewing, miyāʼoṅ).

ety: mana+S. कः
def: s.m. (rustic) Mind; soul; life (i.q. man, q.v.).
s.m. = manuʼā, q.v.
corr. of manuj, q.v.s.v. منو manu.
adj. & s.m. Captivating the mind or heart, &c. (i.q. man-hārī, q.v.s.v. man); — s.f. (for S. mano-hāri), What captivates the mind, or charms, fascinates or delights, &c.
s.m. (dialec.) = mānukh = mānush, q.v.

ety: manu+ja
def: s.m. See s.v. منو manu.
s.m. = manush = manushya, qq.v.
s.m. corr. of mānush, q.v.

ety: Prk. मणूसो; S. मनुष्यः
def: s.m. (dialec.) = manushya and mānus, qq.v.
wety: Ultimately from Arabic مَأْنُوس (maʔnūs).

ety: Prk. माणुसो = S. मानुषः
def: s.m. A human being, a man; — husband.
wety: From Sanskrit मनुष्य (manuṣya), likely from a derivative of Proto-Indo-European *mon- (“man”).

ety: pass. part. of أنس 'to be sociable, or familiar,' &c.
def: part. adj. Associated; attached, friendly, familiar, intimate; — cheering, solacing, gladdening (to, -kā); — s.m. A companion, associate, friend, intimate, familiar.

ety: Prk. मणूसअइआ; S. मनुष्यता+इका
def: s.f. The state or condition of man; manhood, manliness, humanity; — (dialec.) any object of human pursuit (i.q. purushārth, q.v.)
subs: (bhal = bhalā, q.v.), s.f. Humanity; goodness; urbanity; honourableness, gentlemanliness.

ety: S. मनुष्यः, q.v.; Vedic मनुषः
def: s.m. A man; — a husband

ety: fr. manus
def: adj. & s.m. Belonging to mankind; appertaining to men; human; — a human being, a man; a person, an individual (vulg. mānus or mānas, q.v.)
s.f. A woman.
s.f. A woman; a wife.
s.m. The being a man, the condition or the quality of a human being; human nature, humanity; manliness; man's estate, a man.
adj. Human; — s.m. 'An offspring of Manu,' a human being, a man, mortal, being, person, individual; mankind (syn. ādamī, vulg. ādmī)
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Man-slaying, homicidal; one who slays a human being, a homicide, a murderer
s.m. The human form; — human existence
s.m. The human body, the human form; mortal flesh; — manushya-tan pānā or dharnā, To get, or to assume, a human body, to become incarnate
s.f. Traffic in human beings; slave-trade
part. adj. (f. -ā), Formed or made by man
s.m. Mankind, the human race; men, people.

def: s.f. = S منشيتو मनुष्यत्व manushyatva, s.m. (fr. manushya, q.v.), The state or condition of man, manhood, humanity; manliness.
s.m. = S منشيتا मनुष्यता manushyatā, s.f. (fr. manushya, q.v.), The state or condition of man, manhood, humanity; manliness.
adj. Thinking, conceiving; minding, regarding.

ety: caus. of manānā, or doub. caus. of mannā
def: v.t. To cause to conciliate, or to be conciliated, &c. (see manānā).
s.m. See s.v. manu.
adj. (f. -ā), To be honoured or respected; worthy of honour; honourable, venerable, estimable, respectable; to be believed or credited, credible
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -matī), Honoured, venerated, respected, esteemed; — an estimable person, &c.; — mānyā, s.f. An estimable woman; a highly respected woman.

def: s.f. = S مانيتو मान्यत्व mānyatva, s.m. (fr. mānya), Honourableness, venerableness, estimableness, worthiness, respectability; honour; credibility.
s.m. = S مانيتا मान्यता mānyatā, s.f. (fr. mānya), Honourableness, venerableness, estimableness, worthiness, respectability; honour; credibility.

ety: n. of place fr. نظر 'to see, behold,' &c.
def: s.m. Countenance, face, aspect, visage, looks; — an object of sight, a sight, a view; a landscape; a show, spectacle, theatre, scene
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَنْظَر (manzar), from Arabic مَنْظَر (manẓar).

ety: n. of place fr. نزل 'to alight, to descend,' &c.
def: s.f. A place for alighting, a place for the accommodation of travellers, a caravansary, an inn, a hotel; a house, lodging, dwelling, mansion, habitation, station; a mansion of the moon; story or floor (of a house); deck (of a ship); — a day's journey; — a stage (in travelling, or in the divine life); — place of destination, goal; boundary, end, limit
subs: adv. From stage to stage; by stages; — at every atage
v.t. To convey (one) to his destination; to convey (one) to his last resting-place, to perform the obsequies of; — to bring (a thing) home to (a person), to prove to
(-ko), To lay down (a corpse) for a while on the way to its resting-place
or manzil karnā, To finish a journey; to get over a stage; to go one stage
v.n. To finish a difficult task
s.f. The goal of desire; goal, destination
To reach (one's) destination; — to gain (one's) object
A stiff journey or stage; a long stage or march.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَنزِل (manzil), from Arabic مَنْزِل (manzil, “house, dwelling”). First attested in c. 1421 as Old Hindi مَنْزِل (mnzl /⁠manzil⁠/, “abode”).

ety: for A. منزلة manzilat, fr. manzil
def: s.m. A story (of a house, — used in comp. to form adj., e.g. do-manzila, 'two-storied')
subs: In the degree or position (of); in the place (of), instead (of), as.

ety: for A. منزلة manzilat, fr. manzil
def: s.m. A story (of a house, — used in comp. to form adj., e.g. do-manzila, 'two-storied')
subs: In the degree or position (of); in the place (of), instead (of), as.

ety: pass. part. of نظم 'to arrange in order,' &c.
def: part. adj. Arranged in order; joined; threaded, strung; — metrical, versified, in verse; poetic
subs: s.m. Anything placed in a series, row, line, or order; — a composition in rhyme or verse; verses; — rhyme.

ety: for A. منظومة manz̤ūmat, fem. of manz̤ūm
def: part. adj. Arranged in order; joined; threaded, strung; — metrical, versified, in verse; poetic
subs: s.m. Anything placed in a series, row, line, or order; — a composition in rhyme or verse; verses; — rhyme.

ety: pass. part. of نظر 'to see, look at,' &c.
def: part. adj. Seen, looked at; visible; admired; — chosen; approved of, admitted, accepted; sanctioned, granted; — agreeable; acceptable; admissible; — designed, intended: manz̤ūr-e-ḵẖudā, Approved of God; — God's will
subs: v.t. To approve, admit, accept; — to agree to; to sanction, to grant; — to confirm; to affirm

adj. Agreeable to the sight (of); chosen; approved; beloved; — s.m. An object of regard or affection, a beloved object; a sweetheart, a mistress; a favourite; a protégé; — pesh-gāh-se manz̤ūr kiyā gayā, Received the assent or sanction of the ruler or master, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian منظور (manzūr), from Arabic مَنْظُور (manẓūr).

ety: fr. manz̤ūr
def: s.f. Approval; choice; — admission; consent, permission, sanction; — admissibility
subs: or manz̤ūrī bā-ẓimnan, Tacit or implied consent
To be expecting, or to await, sanction
A Government appointment conveying a retiring pension
adv. With the approval, or consent, or sanction (of, -ě)
On condition of approval, &c.; subject to the approval (of, -ě).

ety: Prk. माउडिआ; S. मातृ+ल+इका
def: s.f. (dialec.) Mother, &c. ( = mā, or māʼī).
s.m. (f. -ī) = موسا mausā, q.v.

ety: fr. māpnā, q.v.
def: s.m. Measurement; mensuration; — a system of measures and weights
subs: adj. Of full or standard measure, or height, or size.

ety: perf. part. of māpnā, q.v.
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Measured; surveyed
subs: 'Measured soup with counted lumps (of meat)'; scarcity of provision; — penurious economy.

def: adj. & s.m. Serving for measuring or weighing; determining by measure, &c.; — a measurer; a surveyor; — (in Geom.) a gnomon.
s.m. Measuring; — a pair of scales, a balance.

ety: i.q. mapānā, q.v.
def: s.m. Measuring; measure; quantity.

ety: caus. of māpnā, q.v., and = māp˚+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To cause to measure.

ety: map(nā) + at = Prk. अत्ती = S. आप्तिः (āpi, caus. augment+aff. ति)
def: s.f. Measure
subs: s.f. Idem (and the more com. form).

ety: S. मायि+फलं
def: s.m. Gall-nut (syn. mājū-phal, q.v.).
adj. corr. of موافق muwāfiq, q.v.
adj. & s.f. (dialec.; prob. for mātī) = mat-wālī, fem. of mat-wālā, q.v.

ety: māp˚ = Prk. माप्प(इ) = S. माप्य(ते), caus. pass. fr. rt. मा (used actively)+nā = अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To measure; to survey

ety: fr. the trans. māpnā
def: v.n. To be measured; — v.t. = māpnā, q.v.

ety: doub. caus. of māpnā; and = māp˚+āw+āw, &c.; see mapānā
def: v.t. To cause to be measured, to have or get (a thing) measured.

ety: conj. part. of mārnā, and = Prk. मारिअ or मारि; S. मारयित्वा
def: part. Having beaten, &c. (see mārnā; — mār is frequently prefixed adverbially to other verbs, and intensifies or otherwise affects their signification, as will be seen from the following examples)
subs: v.t. To beat and drive away; to put to flight
v.t. To lay dead, to slay, kill; — to withhold (wages, &c.) wrongfully
v.n. To live by plunder or spoliation, to earn by robbery, to prey (upon)
v.t. To knock down; to complete the destruction of
(-ko), To take or obtain by plunder or robbery, to plunder, rob
v.n. 'To smite oneself and die,' to commit suicide; — to slay (some of the enemy) and die, to do and die (also mārke marnā)
v.t. To beat and drive back, to beat back; to overthrow.

ety: Pehl. mār; Zend māra, rt. mar; S. मार, rt. मर्
def: s.m. A snake, serpent
subs: s.f. Serpentine twisting and winding; circumvolution; crooked manœuvring, crafty device, intrigue, chicanery

ety: conj. part. of marnā; and = Prk. मरिअ = S. मृत्वा, rt. मृ
def: part. Having died, &c. (frequent as first member of verbal compounds, as mar-paćnā, &c., q.v.s.v. marnā).

ety: fr. mār-nā, q.v.
def: s.f. Beating, striking; — a beating, a blow, stroke; punishment; affliction; — battle; a firing (at), cannonading, bombarding; — a rushing upon, a crowding round; — plunder, extortion, exaction; — cure, remedy (e.g. khūjlī-kī mār ghī hai); — lure, temptation, snare; — a kind of stiff clay or loamy soil with sand and vegetable mould; a rich black soil
subs: (-par), A beating (or punishment) to fall (upon); to be beaten
mār-piṭāʼī, mār-dhār, or mār-kuṭāʼī, s.f. Beating and bruising; assault and battery; fight, affray, struggle
(-ko), To give a beating (to), to beat, to smite
adj. & s.m. Quarrelsome, turbulent; — a turbulent fellow, a brawler
v.n. To suffer a beating, to get a drubbing, to be beaten (by, -se)
(-ko), To get (another) a beating, to cause (one) to suffer punishment, to have (one) punished; to punish
To go in plunder or spoliation, &c., to be made away with.

ety: see mar-nā
def: s.m. Dying.

ety: mār, q.v. + Prk. अओ = S. अ+कः
def: s.m. A striking, beating; a blow, &c. ( = mār); an attack (as on a fort, &c.), bombarding, &c.; a line (of fire); range or reach (of fire); — a violent and general destruction; — overwhelming profusion or plenty (of anything)
subs: s.f. Beating and buffeting, &c. ( = mār-kuṭāʼī, q.v.s.v. mār)

or mārā-mārī, s.f. Mutual beating or striking; contention, struggle, scuffle, broil, fray; — jostling; bustling; rush, crush; varied and toilsome efforts (as after the means of subsistence, &c.); — exceeding plentifulness (of); — preposterœ venis usus reciprocus: mārā-mār karnā, To bustle vehemently about; to make all haste; to toil hard; to try one's best

ety: perf. part. of mārnā, and = Prk. मारिअओ = S. मारित+कः
def: part. Beaten, smitten; struck down; killed, slain; destroyed, ruined, spoilt; foundered or overturned (as a boat, &c.); cut off; lost, &c. (see mārnā); — s.m. One who is beaten or stricken, &c. (by); a victim
subs: v.n. To lie (or be) killed or slain; to lie ruined or destroyed (as a field of corn, &c.)

v. pass. To be killed or slain; — to be overcome; — to be destroyed, or ruined, &c.; — to be overturned or sunk (as a boat, &c.); — to be cut off (as a caravan, or a detachment, &c.); to be lost, to be not forthcoming
or mārā phirnā, v.n. To wander about; — to be knocked about

ety: perf. part. of marnā, q.v.; — and = Prk. मलिदओ, or मलिअओ; S. मर्(मृ)+इत+कः
def: part. (f. -ī), Dead; dried up, withered; half-dead-and-alive; poor, wretched, miserable, &c.; — s.m. A corpse; carcass; a half-dead-and-alive person, a miserable creature; — base metal
subs: v.n. To move or go along at a half-dead-and-alive pace, to creep along
lit. 'A dead louse'; a miserably distressed, or wretchedly poor person; — a very slow person.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Killing, slaying, destroying; — killer, destroyer (as last member of compounds); — death; pestilence, plague, epidemic disease; — the god of love (Kāmdev); the passion of love; — slaying, killing; — hindrance, impediment, obstruction
subs: (˚ra+āt˚), adj. & s.m. Essentially destructive, murderous, fatal, deadly; of a cruel disposition; — a cruel person, &c.

ety: dialec. form of mārā, p.p. of mārnā
def: adj. (f. -ī), Weak, frail, lean, thin; poor, worthless, bad; incompetent; trifling; mean, low, miserable, churlish
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. māṛī-sī), Little; — a little, a trifle.

ety: n. of place fr. أرب 'to be in want' (of
def: s.m. The time or place for any necessary business.

ety: for A. مربة, inf. n. of ربّ 'to be lord over; to have command or authority over'
def: s.f. Authority, rule, dominion.
s.m.(?) Obstinacy, perverseness (perhaps for maradd; see radd).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مرد (mard).

def: s.m. pl. (of mirfaq, q.v.), Things by which one profits, or gains advantage or benefit; — appurtenances or conveniences of a house (as the kitchen, sink, water-closet, and the like); — elbows, or elbow-joints.
s.m. pl. (of marḥala, q.v.), Stations, stages, days' journeys; journeys.
s.f. pl. (of marḥamat, q.v.), Compassions; favours, kindnesses; gifts.

r: maraiṭhā, mar˚, mal, nā, aiṭhā, aiṅṭhā, aiṅṭhnā
ety: mar˚, prob. = mal(nā
def: part. Twisted; wound up; bound round.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Killing, slaying, putting to death; — killer, slayer, destroyer, executioner; murderer; — any deadly or pestilential disease, plague, pestilence; — a falcon, hawk.
s.m. An epidemic, a pestilential disease, contagious disorder; a plague; murrain; mortality.
s.m. = مرك marak, q.v.

ety: for muṛak, fr. muṛaknā = murakna, q.v.; — cf. muṛnā
def: s.f. A turn, twist, bend, curl (as of a leaf, &c.), convolution; curvature, crookedness; — a fracture, a crack; — affectation; swagger, strut; pride, conceit; — a trick, manœuvre, artifice; — a formula or special rule (for the solution of a difficulty).

ety: for A. معرکة maʻrakat, n. of place fr. عرك 'to rub, press, scrape,' &c.
def: s.m. A place, or scene, of battle or fight; field of battle; battle-ground; an ampitheatre (for gladiators or prize-fighters); — strife, fight
subs: adj. (f. -kī), 'Martial, warlike'; — of importance; important, momentous
A man of importance, a great man

ety: for A. مرقة
def: s.f. A kind of broth; a quantity of broth.

ety: fr. marakata
def: adj. (f. -ī), Emerald-like; of the colour of an emerald; of or belonging to an emerald; having the properties of an emerald.
s.m. An emerald.

adj. Soft, mild, bland; tender; — s.m. A species of goose (with red legs and bill); a duck; a horse; a grove of pomegranate trees; a cloud; lampblack (used as a collyrium); — a villain, rogue, scoundrel.

ety: S. मुरलः
def: s.m.f. A kind of (river) fish.

ety: for A. مرمة, inf. n. of رمّ 'to repair, rectify, mend,' &c.
def: s.f. Repairing, rectifying, mending; reparation, repair; rectification, amendment
subs: adj. Wanting repair, in need of repairs, out of order; broken
v.t. To get (a house, or a thing) repaired
v.t. To repair, mend; rectify, set or put right.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مرمت (marammat), Arabic مَرَمَّة (maramma). Compare Punjabi مُرَمَّت (murammat) and Nepali मर्मत (marmat).

ety: fr. marammat
def: adj. Repaired, mended; — in need of repair; out of order; broken.
s.m. Killing, slaying, slaughter.

ety: see marnā
def: s.m. Dying; death; dying with shame; — ruin, destruction
subs: s.f. Place of death, mortal spot, vital part (of the body)
or maran-kāl, s.m. Time of death, the dying moments.

ety: caus. of mārnā, q.v.
def: v.t. To suffer sodomy (the word gāṅṛ being usually expressed).
adj. See s.v. marnā.
s.f.(?) The pit of an old well that has gone to ruins.
s.f. pl. (of مرسة marasat, rt. مرس), Ropes.

ety: contrac. of marāsīm, pl. of marsūm, q.v.
def: s.m. Marks, stamps, impressions; signs, characters; traces, vestiges; — prescripts, commands, orders, biddings, injunctions, laws; assignments, appointments; obligations, observances, duties; customs; conventions, pactions, conditions, agreements.

ety: martaba, q.v.
def: s.m. Preliminary stages; preliminary measures
subs: (-ke), To pass through the different stages (of).

def: s.m. pl. (of marātib), Affairs, matters, particulars, &c.
adj. See s.v. māra or mār.

ety: marā(nā) + ū = Prk. इउओ = S. (इ)तृ+कः
def: s.m. One who suffers sodomy, a catamite (see marānā).
s.m. = mālaw, q.v.
s.f. pl. (of mirʼāt, q.v.), Looking-glasses, mirrors.

ety: inf. n. of مرض 'to fall sick,' &c.
def: s.m. Disease, sickness, indisposition, illness, ailment, malady, distemper
subs: s.m. Mortal disease; the last illness
To avert disease; to cure the malady (of, -kā)
s.m. An obstinate disease; a troublesome or painful complaint
'Of what disease is it the medicine?' of what use is it? what is it good for?
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَرَض (maraz), from Arabic مَرَض (maraḍ).

ety: rel. n. fr. maraẓ
def: adj. (dialec.) Sick, ill, diseased, unwell (see marīẓ).

r: mar-bhūkā, mar-bhukkā, mar-bhukkhā, mar-bhūkhā, mar, marnā, bhūkhā
ety: mar, conj. part. of marnā + bhūkhā, qq.v.
def: adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Starved to death, famished; greedy, voracious, ravenous; — a famished person, a starveling; a greedy person, &c.

r: mar-bhūkhā, mar-bhukkā, mar-bhukkhā, mar-bhūkā, mar, marnā, bhūkhā
ety: mar, conj. part. of marnā + bhūkhā, qq.v.
def: adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Starved to death, famished; greedy, voracious, ravenous; — a famished person, a starveling; a greedy person, &c.

r: mar-bhukkā, mar-bhukkhā, mar-bhūkā, mar-bhūkhā, mar, marnā, bhūkhā
ety: mar, conj. part. of marnā + bhūkhā, qq.v.
def: adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Starved to death, famished; greedy, voracious, ravenous; — a famished person, a starveling; a greedy person, &c.

r: mar-bhukkhā, mar-bhukkā, mar-bhūkā, mar-bhūkhā, mar, marnā, bhūkhā
ety: mar, conj. part. of marnā + bhūkhā, qq.v.
def: adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Starved to death, famished; greedy, voracious, ravenous; — a famished person, a starveling; a greedy person, &c.

ety: pass. part. of ربط 'to tie, bind, make fast'
def: part. Tied, bound, made fast, fastened, attached; closed; — well composed or put together, consistent (as a book); concordant (in Grammar); polished, classical, or grammatical (language).

ety: Pehl. mard, mart; Zend mareta; S. मर्त्य
def: s.m. A man; a male; a husband; — a brave or valiant man, a hero
subs: vulg. marde-ādmī, s.m. A gentleman; — a form of address; my good Sir
adj. & s.f. Lascivious, lustful (woman); — an adultress, a strumpet
s.m. The son of a brave man
adj. & s.m. Heroic, valiant; — one who acts valiantly, a hero
Not to see the face of a man; to have nothing to do with the male sex, to live single (a woman)
To be dragged into a quarrel (men).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مرد (mard).

ety: mard, q.v.+dim. aff. ak = S. क
def: s.m. A little man, a manikin; — a low or contemptible fellow; a puppy; a rascal.
adj. (f. -ā), Crushing, grinding, pounding, breaking; subduing; destroying; — s.m. Rubbing; anointing; touching; friction; — crushing crumbling, grinding, pounding, bruising; trampling, treading down
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit मर्दन (mardana).

def: s.m. pl. (of mard), Men; brave men, heroes, warriors.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مردان (mardān) ("men").

ety: mard, q.v.+āna = Zend āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. (f. -ī) & adv. Man-like; manly, brave; — male; of, or pertaining to, or suitable to, a man; man's, men's (e.g. mardānī ćāl; mardāna makān); — like a man, manfully, courageously, vigorously
subs: A suit of clothes for a man.

ety: abst. s. fr. next
def: s.f. Manliness, bravery, courage, prowess
subs: s.m. pl. Brave deeds, heroic achievements.

def: s.f. A masculine woman; a brave woman.

ety: mardan ( = mardnā, q.v.)+Prk. इओ = S. इकः (इन्+कः)
def: s.m. One whose business it is to rub unguents, or perfumed paste, &c. over the body; one who anoints (pl. mardaniyāṅ).

ety: mardan ( = mardnā, q.v.)+Prk. इओ = S. इकः (इन्+कः)
def: s.m. One whose business it is to rub unguents, or perfumed paste, &c. over the body; one who anoints (pl. mardaniyāṅ).

ety: mard, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Manhood; virility; manliness; valour.
(dialec.), past conj. part. of mardnā, q.v.
part. Rubbed; kneaded; squeezed; — touched, anointed; — crushed, pounded, ground, bruised, broken; subdued; destroyed.

ety: mard˚ = S. मर्द(ति), rt. मृद्
def: v.t. To rub, touch, anoint; to grind, pound, crush, pulverize, bruise, break; to subdue; to destroy.

ety: P. mard, q.v.+Prk. अओ = S. क+कः
def: s.m. (gen. used by way of contempt, except by women), A man; a male ( = mard); a manikin ( = mardak); — wight; fellow; chap.

ety: pass. part. of ردّ 'to return; to reject,' &c.
def: part. & s.m. Returned, restored; rejected; thrown back; repulsed; refuted; confuted; excluded; reprobated; abandoned, outcast; — a reprobate; an outcast; an apostate; a wretch.

ety: for A. مردودة mardūdat, fem. of mardūd
def: part. adj. = mardūd.

ety: fr. mardūd
def: s.f. Rejection, repulse; — apostasy; — the state of one rejected, &c.

ety: fr. mard, q.v.
def: s.m. A man; men, people; — a polite or civilized man; — pupil (of the eye); — adj. Civil, humane
subs: adj. & s.m. Man-tormenting, man-oppressing; — an oppressor
s.f. Injuring men, oppression, tyranny; robbery and murder
adj. & s.m. Man-devouring; cruel; — a cannibal; — a tyrant, a cruel man
adj. Producing men, or good men
adj. & s.m. Man-tearing, man-rending; rapacious; — who or what tears to pieces and devours mankind
s.f. Numbering of the people, a census

ety: mardum+dim. aff. ak = S. क
def: s.f. Pupil (of the eye).
s.m. pl. (of mardum), People, men and women in general.

ety: mardum+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Manliness, bravery (syn. mardānagī); — humanity, generosity; courtesy, politeness, civility.

r: māre, mārā, ke, ḍar-ke māre, māre ḍar-ke
def: (oblique form of part. mārā, q.v.), postpn. 'Being stricken with,' for, through, because (of, -ke), by reason (of), on account (of), in consequence (of), for the sake (of; — māre may also precede the word it governs, e.g. ḍar-ke māre, or māre ḍar-ke, 'through fear').
s.m. A kind of drum (syn. ḍhol).
s.m. = مرفا marfā, q.v.

ety: pass. part. of رفع 'to raise, elevate,' &c.
def: part. Raised, elevated; exalted; honoured; of high estimation, highly valued; — marked with the vowel-point ẓamm ( u).

ety: Pehl. marg; Zend mahrka, rt. mareñć, fr. mar = S. मर् (मृ)
def: s.f. Death
subs: or marg-ě-mufājāt, or marg-ě-nāgahānī, Sudden death
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَرْگ (marg).

def: s.m. Search, investigation, inquiry; trace, track; road, course, path, way, highway; passage, channel; the intestinal canal, the anus; — way, method; — cure, remedy, antidote; — a saw-kerf; — doctrine, creed, belief
subs: s.m. (f. -ā), Lord or sovereign of the way
s.m. (f. -ī), Guardian of the road, custos viarum; patrol, watchman
(f. -ikā), or mārg-darśī (f. -iṇī), adj. & s.m. Showing the way, guiding; — way-shower, guide, conductor
s.f. 'Taking the road,' journeying, marching, proceeding
wety: Sanskrit मार्ग (mārga). Doublet of مانگ (māṅg).

def: adj. & s.m. Fried (fish); — fried fish.
s.m. Inquiry, search; research; — begging, solicitation; — a beggar, solicitor, suppliant, mendicant; — an arrow.
s.m. The dry bed of a river.

ety: fr. mr̤iga-śiras
def: s.m. The Hindū month corresponding to Nov.-Dec. (in which the full moon enters the constellation Mr̤iga-śiras.)

ety: fr. mr̤iga-śiras
def: s.m. The Hindū month corresponding to Nov.-Dec. (in which the full moon enters the constellation Mr̤iga-śiras.)

ety: mar, or marā+ghāṭ, qq.v.
def: s.m. A place where Hindūs burn their dead, ustrinum.

ety: S. मार्गिकः
def: s.m. 'One who goes the road' (lit. or spiritually); a traveller, wayfarer; a pilgrim; a disciple, follower.
v.n. (Dakh.) To trill, to quaver; to warble, sing; to talk (as birds).
s.f. The fifteenth day of the first half of the month Mārgaśīrsha (the day on which the full moon enters the constellation Mr̤iga-śiras).

ety: pass. part. of رغب 'to desire.' &c.
def: part. Desired; — desirable; agreeable; amiable; lovely, beautiful; estimable, excellent.

ety: for A. مرغوبة marg̠ūbat, fem. of marg̠ūt
def: part. = marg̠ūb, q.v.

s.m. Twists and turns; locks twisted, plaited, or curling; — crisp, curly hair; — modulation or raising of the voice (in singing); quavering; warbling, singing; a trill, shake, quaver; — a crenated arch.

s.m. Twists and turns; locks twisted, plaited, or curling; — crisp, curly hair; — modulation or raising of the voice (in singing); quavering; warbling, singing; a trill, shake, quaver; — a crenated arch.

ety: marg̠, 'a kind of grass; pasture,'+zār, q.v.
def: s.m. A place abounding in verdure or in pasture; a meadow.
s.m. = maḍhā, q.v.

ety: perf. part. of maṛhnā; — and = Prk. मड्ढओ; S. मृष्ट+कः
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Covered; lined; encased; headed, topped; gilt.

ety: inf. n. of رحب 'to be ample, spacious, or roomy'
def: s.m. Ampleness, amplitude, largeness, spaciousness, roominess.

ety: for marḥabaṉ, acc. of marḥab, governed by a verb understood
def: intj. Welcome to ampleness, or to a spacious or roomy place; welcome! hail! God bless you! bravo!
adj. & s.f. = marahṭī, q.v.
s.f. = maḍhaiyā, and maṛhī, qq.v.

ety: for A. مرحلة marḥalat, n. of place fr. رحل 'to remove, depart, journey,' &c.
def: s.m. A day's journey, a stage; — the place or time of travelling; a place of alighting or abode; a halting-place, or station, or inn (for travellers); — a battery; bastion (of a fort)
subs: s.m. The watchman of the road between two halting-places or stations.

ety: A. also marham, fr. the Persian
def: s.f. Dressing or binding up (a wound); applying salve; — repair, renovation
subs: (-kī), To dress or bind up (a wound), to apply salve (to); — to repair, put in order, set right
s.m. One who applies dressing or salve (to a wound), dresser (of a wound).

ety: for A. مرحمة inf. n. of رحم 'to have mercy, or pity, or compassion,' &c.
def: s.f. Mercy, pity, compassion, tenderness of heart; inclination to favour; favour; clemency; pardon, forgiveness (syn. raḥmat); — a present
subs: (-ko), To favour with, to vouchsafe, bestow, grant.

ety: i.q. maṛhnā
def: s.f. Covering; lining; heading (of a drum, &c.).

ety: Prk. मरहट्ठिओ and मरहट्ठिआ; S. महाराष्ट्रिकः and महाराष्ट्रिका
def: adj. & s.m. Belonging to the Mahrattā country; — a native of that country, a Mahratā; — s.f. The language of the Mahratās, — maladministration under the Mahratās; misrule; rapine, plunder; — name of a class of songs
subs: To behave in the manner of the Mahratās, to plunder, ravage.

ety: Prk. मरहट्ठओ; S. महाराष्ट्र+कः
def: s.m. A Marhattā.

ety: Prk. मढिआ; S. मठ+इका
def: s.f. A small temple; — a cottage, a hut; a small shed; a jogī's hut or abode, a hermit's cell.

ety: maṛh˚ = Prk. मड्ड(इ), or मढ(इ), fr. मड्ड = मड्ढ = S. मृष्ट, p.p.p. of rt. मृद्
def: v.t. To cover (as a book, &c. with leather, or a drum with parchment), to overspread; to case, to encase (in), to coat over; to gild; to put a top or head to; to encrust; — to throw (upon), to saddle (one, with).

ety: pass. part. of رهب 'to fear'
def: part. adj. Feared, dreaded; — fearful, dreadful, terrible.

ety: pass. part. of رحم 'to have mercy,' &c.
def: part. & s.m. On whom God has had mercy, or who has found mercy; deceased; the late; — the object of God's mercy, the deceased, the defunct of happy memory.

ety: for A. مرحومة marḥūmat, fem. of marḥūm
def: part. & s.f. = marḥūm.

ety: pass. part. of رهن 'to deposit (an article
def: part. Deposited as a pledge, pledged, pawned, mortgaged
s.m. (rustic) = maḍhā, and maṅḍhwā, qq.v.

ety: mār, q.v.+Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. Beating; blow, &c. (see mār and mārā)
subs: (-par), To be cozened, or swindled, or done

def: s.f. Killing, slaying; ruin; — any deadly disease, plestilence, &c. (see (next).

ety: Prk. मारिआ; S. मारिका
def: s.f. Deadly disease, plague, pestilence, epidemic; murrain; — mortality; — arduous toil, hard labour.
s.f. Rice-water; — starch ( = māṅḍī, q.v.).

ety: S. मद+आद्या
def: s.f. (dialec.) The Caryota palm, Caryota urens; — palm-wine or toddy (drawn from that tree).

ety: pass. part. of رأي 'to see'
def: part. adj. Seen; visible.

ety: v.n. fr. مرأ 'to taste' (food
def: s.f. The gullet, the œsophagus (the forms murī and mirrī are corruptions).

ety: S. मर्या
def: s.f. A border; a bank; division; a small field or patch of sown ground; a bed, plot, or parterre (in a garden).
s.f. contrac. of mahāṛī, q.v.

ety: S. मारिका; cf. marak
def: s.f. A plague, pestilence, an epidemic; cholera morbus; murrain
subs: v.n. A plague or pestilence to occur
(fem. marī-lī), adj. Plague-stricken; — fit for death (peculiar to the language of women).

def: s.m. = marić or mirć, q.v.

ety: S. मरीचिः or मारीचः
def: s.m. Name of the demon employed by Rāvaṇ to entice away Sītā.
s.m. (dialec.) = mirćā = marić or mirć, q.v.

s.m.f. A ray of light, sunbeam, moonbeam; — s.m. Name of a Prajā-pati, the first of the ten 'lords of created beings,' and son of Brahmā; — name of one of the seven Rishis represented by the seven stars in Ursa Major; — a miser, a niggard
s.f. = marīćikā, q.v.
s.f. The mirage.

ety: v.n. fr. مرد 'to rub,' &c.
def: adj. Stubborn, obstinate, perverse, contumacious, disobedient, rebellious, insolent, proud.

ety: for A. معرفة maʻrifat, q.v.
def: s.m. A determinate noun, a definite or proper noun.

ety: for A. معرفة, inf. n. of عرف 'to know'
def: s.f. Knowledge, cognition, cognizance, acquaintance; — science; learning; — art, skill, craft; — means, cause, reason, account; — postpn. By means (of, -kī), through the medium (of), through; by the hands (of), by; in charge (of); to the care (of).

ety: rt. مرس
def: s.f. Mode, manner, habit, custom, fashion.
s.m. An honourable man, a respectable or venerable person; (in dramatic language) the manager of a theatre; — a kind of pot-herb or vegetable, Amaranthus oleraceus (syn. māṭ or māṭh).
s.m. Mustard-seed, Sinapis dichotoma.
part. (f. -ā), Killed, slain, destroyed, put to death, slaughtered.
adj. & s.m. Lean, thin, &c. ( = māṛā, q.v.); — a lean man, &c.

ety: Prk. मलिअ+डो, or अल्लो; S. मर् (मृ)+इत+लः or अलः
def: adj. & s.m. Half-dead, having no life (in him, or it, &c.), more dead than alive, moribund; — lean, emaciated, much reduced (by sickness, &c.); weak, feeble; — lethargic, indolent, lazy, slow, inactive; — a half-dead-and-alive person; a lean or emaciated person, &c.

ety: māṅḍī, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि caus. augment) + nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To paste; to stick on.

ety: fr. maraẓ, q.v.
def: adj. & s.m. Sick, ill, diseased; infirm; — a sick person, a patient.

ety: n. of place fr. عرض 'to appear; to occur,' &c.
def: s.m. Place of the appearance, or occurrence, or manifestation (of a thing); scene (of); place of meeting; — the case of the appearance, &c. (of a thing); — occurrence; occasion (of); contingency.

ety: for A. مريضة marīẓat, fem. of marīẓ
def: adj. & s.f. = marīẓ, q.v.

ety: noun of place, fr. رجع 'to return,' &c.
def: s.m. A place to which a person or thing returns; asylum, place or refuge; a rendezvous; time of return; — the place or thing to which a person or thing is referred (as his or its source); place of reference; (in Gram.) an antecedent; — the place, or the state or condition, or the result, to which a person or thing ultimately comes; a goal.
= H مرجادا मरजादा marjādā, s.f. see maryād, and maryādā.
= H مرجاد मरजाद marjād, s.f. see maryād, and maryādā.
adj. = maryādik, q.v.

s.m. Wiping or rubbing off; cleaning, scouring, cleansing; cleansing the person by wiping, or bathing, or rubbing it with unguents; — sprinkling with water for purification before the commencement of religious ceremonies; — clearing or wiping off (a debt); — pardon.
s.m. Coral.
v.n. See s.v. marnā.
